Patch Set 15:

Based on the recent success we could simplify this patch a bit.

I researched the upstream transition from 4.13 to 4.14.

4.13 have a kernel_write() that it not plug compatible with vfs_write, but vfs_write() is exported.

4.14 has a plug compatible kernel() but doesn't export vfs_write() anymore.

The key decider is whether or not the kernel_write() implements the new API.  The LIBCFS_EXPORT_VFS_WRITE and LIBCFS_EXPORT_KERNEL_WRITE can be removed.

All of the code path decisions would then be based on LIBCFS_NEW_KERNEL_WRITE.

Comments?

Patch Set 15:

Sadly distros don't always handle changes in order. I have seen cases in which combinations of changes exist in distros that never existed upstream. Also the window is short before the release. Lets land as is. It does work well now. Thanks for fixing it up.

commit.subject: LU-10560 libcfs: Use kernel_write when appropriate
commit.message:
LU-10560 libcfs: Use kernel_write when appropriate

Changes in the upstream kernel might have removed
vfs_write() in favor of kernel_write().

Unfortunately, the kernel_write() was initially exported
with an API that is not plug compatible with vfs_write()

The ring down is:
- kernel_write new API
- vfs_write
